segments.text: Oscars just ditched [music] TV. In a historic move, the Academy Awards are moving exclusively to YouTube starting
2029. That ends [music] a five decade run on ABC, which has aired the ceremony since 1976. [music]
From 2029 to 2033, YouTube will hold exclusive [music] global streaming rights, not just for the main ceremony,
announcements, [music] and behindthe-scenes events. And for the first time ever, the Oscars will stream live and free worldwide, reaching over
two billion users with multiple language audio tracks [music] and closed captions. Reason: Traditional TV ratings have been falling for years. [music]
Even in 2025, the Oscars drew about 19.7 million viewers, far below their 1990s [music] peak. And here's where it gets
bigger. The same app you use to watch creators and podcasts is now hosting Hollywood's [music] biggest night. So,
if the Oscars can move, what stops the FIFA World Cup, the Olympics, or the NBA? [music] Is this the future of live
events, or the quiet end of television as we [music] know it? Tell me what you think and follow Full Disclosure for
